name: flux-schnell-sparse-next-action
goal: Choose the next controlled Flux Schnell optimization step toward the strongest compatible skill stack.
instructions: Use the auto_deploy ComfyUI role READMEs, active skill registry, and prior benchmark artifacts to optimize combinations of compatible skills, not just a single isolated skill. Treat every method as a parameterized family and search the speed/quality tradeoff frontier with directed parameter moves, not exhaustive grids. The final deliverable is one composed Flux optimization stack, for example tuned sparse attention plus compile plus selective FFN/Linear quantization, with incompatible components explicitly ruled out by measured evidence. The objective is maximum practical acceleration while preserving correctness and required multimodal visual quality.
constraints: Executor must keep prompt, seed, official model hyperparameters, and image metrics fixed unless the user explicitly overrides them.

If no user override is present, Reviewer/Executor/Checker must use official model defaults for resolution, inference steps, guidance/CFG, sampler, scheduler, time shift, and model-specific coefficients.
For the current FLUX.1-schnell handoff, use 1024x1024, 4 inference steps, guidance_scale=0.0, and max_sequence_length=256 unless a model-card/config override is explicitly recorded.
Legacy small-resolution smoke settings such as 512x512 are debug-only and must not be used as acceptance evidence.
When official benchmark configuration changes, Checker must use a fresh run-level baseline artifact for that fixed configuration.
Executor must default to canvas/config/experiment wiring and treat efficient_skill/ plus benchmark/ as restricted edit surfaces.
Executor must put the runnable candidate model behind draft_canvas/flux_schnell_candidate_canvas.py; Checker only evaluates that candidate wrapper.
Checker commands must not include skill-specific implementation details such as sparse attention, quantization recipe, density, topk, apply_to, or compile scope.
Executor may modify restricted edit surfaces only when reviewer_next_action.json explicitly authorizes efficient_skill or benchmark with an allowed reason.
All formal Flux checker runs must use the unified benchmark interface: benchmark/flux_schnell_benchmark.py.
Skill-family benchmark scripts are debug-only and must not be used as acceptance evidence.
Checker benchmark must use a run-level fixed baseline artifact instead of remeasuring baseline for each candidate iteration.
Checker must evaluate speed and quantitative quality from benchmark artifacts.
Checker must run multimodal qualitative review for visual outputs.
Reviewer must consider compatible skill combinations before accepting.
Reviewer must keep parameter-search context visible and should direct more aggressive, more conservative, or intermediate parameter moves based on speed/quality evidence.
Reviewer must not accept only because isolated skills were sampled; a final composed delivery stack must be implemented and benchmarked, or each excluded stack component must have a measured blocker.
success_criteria: reviewer_next_action.json names one concrete next Flux optimization step.
reviewer_next_action.json describes how the step fits a broader compatible skill stack.
reviewer_next_action.json includes exact checker command, required visual quality config, and acceptance criteria.
